# no-buildsubdir

**File:** `build/parsePreamble.cc`

## Problem

RPM 4.20.0 introduced a per-package build subdirectory. When parsing a spec
file, RPM automatically creates a build subdirectory named
`BUILD/<Name>-<Version>-build/` and uses it as the working directory for all
build phases (`%prep`, `%build`, `%install`, etc.). This behavior is present in
both the 4.20.x and 6.x release lines.

The relevant code in
[RPM 6.0.1 `parsePreamble.cc` (lines 1296-1305)](https://github.com/rpm-software-management/rpm/blob/58a917a6c5e24e9e8a01976c17d2eee06249b9b6/build/parsePreamble.cc#L1296-L1305)
and identically in
[RPM 4.20.1 `parsePreamble.c` (lines 1288-1296)](https://github.com/rpm-software-management/rpm/blob/c8dc5ea575a2e9c1488036d12f4b75f6a5a49120/build/parsePreamble.c#L1288-L1296):

```cpp
if (!spec->buildDir) {
    char *bn = rpmExpand("%{NAME}-%{VERSION}-build", NULL);
    for (char *t = bn; *t; t++) {
        if (*t == '^' || *t == '~')
            *t = '_';
    }
    spec->buildDir = rpmExpand("%{_top_builddir}/", bn, NULL);
    free(bn);
```

In RPM 4.19.x and earlier, this `spec->buildDir` field did not exist. `%setup`
operated directly in `%{_builddir}` (typically `~/rpmbuild/BUILD/`), creating
only the source subdirectory `%{NAME}-%{VERSION}` within it. See
[RPM 4.19.1 `parsePreamble.c` (lines 1209-1224)](https://github.com/rpm-software-management/rpm/blob/98b301ebb44fb5cabb56fc24bc3aaa437c47c038/build/parsePreamble.c#L1209-L1224)
— there is no `buildDir` setup, only `buildRoot`.

### Why RPM uses a subdirectory

In a traditional RPM workflow, the `%prep` phase (typically `%setup`) extracts a
source tarball into `BUILD/`, the `%build` phase compiles in that directory, and
`%install` copies the results into `BUILDROOT/`. The per-package subdirectory
isolates each package's build from others sharing the same `BUILD/` tree.

### Why it breaks `rules_pkg`

`rules_pkg` uses rpmbuild only for the packaging step — it skips `%prep` and
`%build` entirely. The
[`SetupWorkdir()` function in `make_rpm.py`](https://github.com/bazelbuild/rules_pkg/blob/e15c3316b04d7f363f04afafd160fa634ce4276c/pkg/make_rpm.py#L255-L260)
populates `BUILD/` directly with pre-built files, and the
[`%install` scriptlet generated by `rpm_pfg.bzl`](https://github.com/bazelbuild/rules_pkg/blob/e15c3316b04d7f363f04afafd160fa634ce4276c/pkg/rpm_pfg.bzl#L74-L78)
uses `cp` with source paths relative to `BUILD/` (e.g. `cp 'tests/rpm/hello.txt' ...`).
When RPM switches the working directory to the `<Name>-<Version>-build/`
subdirectory, the `%install` scriptlet can no longer find the source files that
`SetupWorkdir()` placed in `BUILD/` — resulting in errors like:

```
cp: cannot stat 'tests/rpm/hello.txt': No such file or directory
```

## Fix

The patch sets `spec->buildDir` to `%{_top_builddir}` directly, reverting to
the pre-4.20 behavior where the top-level `BUILD/` directory is the build
directory. This ensures that files placed in `BUILD/` by external tools like
`make_rpm.py` are visible during `%install`.
